findings.description: Actual download-worker failures bypass ErrorLog. DefaultModelRegistry.download() callbackFlow receives ModelDownloadStatus updates (including FAILED with non-empty errorMessage) from DownloadRepository, but never routes FAILED into errorLog.e("download", ...). Only startup scan / allowlist load / resumePartialDownloads failures hit ErrorLog for the `download` component. User-spec § 'Проверка лога ошибок' explicitly maps the `download` component to 'отключить Wi-Fi во время скачивания' scenario — that path currently produces a Log.e(TAG, ...) entry in logcat from DownloadWorker.kt:276 only, no errors.log entry. AC-16 is желательный (non-blocking), so severity is medium rather than high.
findings.recommendation: In DefaultModelRegistry.download() callbackFlow, after updateEntry/trySend, add: if (status.status == ModelDownloadStatusType.FAILED) scope.launch { errorLog.e(LOG_TAG_DOWNLOAD, "download ${model.name} failed: ${status.errorMessage}") }. Keep the existing Gallery Log.e in DownloadWorker as-is (inner copy-paste scope).

findings.description: ErrorLog missing from CoreRuntimeModule @Provides list. Tech-spec Architecture § Shared resources and § `CoreRuntimeModule` explicitly states: 'Provides `DownloadRepository`, `LlmModelHelper`, `ModelRegistry`, `ErrorLog`'. Current CoreRuntimeModule declares only the first three as @Provides @Singleton. ErrorLog is annotated `@Singleton class ErrorLog @Inject constructor(...)` so Hilt auto-binds it — functionally equivalent scoping. But the contract in tech-spec Architecture was explicit, and Task 5 decisions.md knowingly deviated ('Hilt auto-binds через constructor injection'). Since ErrorLog IS still a process-wide singleton, this is a spec-text vs code drift rather than a resource-leak bug.
findings.recommendation: Either add `@Provides @Singleton fun provideErrorLog(@ApplicationContext ctx: Context): ErrorLog = ErrorLog(ctx)` (and drop the `@Singleton` + `@Inject` from the class, keeping it ordinary) to match tech-spec literally, or update tech-spec Architecture § Shared resources row for ErrorLog to say 'constructor-injected @Singleton' and remove it from CoreRuntimeModule's provides list. Pick one source of truth.

findings.description: ClassNotFoundException branch in sendNotification uses Log.e only — no ErrorLog. This branch fires when :app forgot to set DefaultDownloadRepository.mainActivityFqn or set it to a bogus class; the user-visible effect is silent notification loss. Gallery-extracted file (Google LLC copyright header) so the task's Edge cases note `Gallery-extracted, минимальный scope` softens this — but the path exists only in our modified T6 code, not in original Gallery.
findings.recommendation: Inject ErrorLog into DefaultDownloadRepository (add `private val errorLog: ErrorLog` ctor param + update CoreRuntimeModule.provideDownloadRepository) and replace Log.e at line 225 with errorLog.e("download", "MainActivity class not found: $fqn", e). Whitelist `download` covers this setup-error case.

findings.description: BuildConfig.MAIN_ACTIVITY_CLASS_NAME declared but not referenced. TAC-10 mandates this buildConfigField exists (grep target passes), but SanctumApplication.kt:11 hardcodes the string literal: `DefaultDownloadRepository.mainActivityFqn = "app.sanctum.machina.MainActivity"`. The BuildConfig field is dead. Design intent of T6 was 'companion + Data bundle, no BuildConfig'; the field exists only to satisfy the grep-level TAC. Not a bug, but source-of-truth duplication for the FQN string.
findings.recommendation: Replace the string literal in SanctumApplication.onCreate with `DefaultDownloadRepository.mainActivityFqn = BuildConfig.MAIN_ACTIVITY_CLASS_NAME` (requires `import app.sanctum.machina.BuildConfig`). Removes duplication and makes the buildConfigField load-bearing.

findings.description: supportImage/supportAudio plumbed from allowlist despite text-only Phase 1 UI. awaitInitialize passes `supportImage = model.llmSupportImage, supportAudio = model.llmSupportAudio`; for both Phase 1 models these are `true`, which causes LlmChatModelHelper.initialize to build visionBackend + audioBackend. Phase 1 UI is text-only (user-spec Ограничения), so the vision/audio backends consume init time and native memory with no observable benefit. Not a correctness bug — LiteRT init still succeeds — but may widen TTFT gap vs Gallery (AC-14 gate) and inflate PSS (AC-9 cap 4 GB).
findings.recommendation: In DefaultModelRegistry.awaitInitialize, hardcode `supportImage = false, supportAudio = false` for Phase 1 (keep the model.llmSupportImage field unchanged for Phase 2 reuse), or add an inline comment documenting the intentional pass-through. Verify in AC-9/AC-14 telemetry whether disabling them closes the TTFT gap.

findings.description: `registry.getModel(modelName) ?: error("Model not initialized")` crashes the UI if registry state changes between Ready-render and send-tap (another VM called cleanup, process restart race, etc.). Kotlin idiom: `error()` throws IllegalStateException on the UI thread, producing a surfaced ANR/crash instead of graceful degradation.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a defensive fallback: `val model = registry.getModel(modelName) ?: run { _uiState.value = ChatUiState.Failed("Model unloaded"); return }`. Preserves happy-path, avoids crashing on a recoverable race.

findings.description: onCleared spawns a standalone `CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.IO)` scope that's never cancelled; while the cleanup coroutine is finite (Job terminates on completion) and the in-method comment documents the rationale (viewModelScope already cancelled), this is GlobalScope-equivalent and any exception thrown by registry.cleanup() is lost. Acceptable for Phase 1 (cleanup path swallows exceptions internally, per decisions.md Task 5 Phase 2 debt note); flagging for Phase 2.
findings.recommendation: Phase 2: move cleanup scope ownership to a ProcessLifecycleOwner-bound singleton (inside :core-runtime) that DefaultModelRegistry can `launch` into, so exceptions surface and are owned by a known Job tree. No action in Phase 1.

findings.description: sizeInBytes for Gemma-4-E4B-it is 3,654,467,584 bytes (~3.4 GB) but user-spec AC-3 and Ограничения describe the model as ~4.3 GB. The allowlist is authoritative per D4 (bundled copy of Gallery's 1_0_11.json) and the JSON value is unchanged from Gallery upstream; the user-spec text is approximate Gallery-inherited lore. Informational only — does not affect any AC except the comparison against the spec's approximate numbers.
findings.recommendation: None for Phase 1. Phase 2: either update user-spec/tech-spec text to '~3.4 GB' or add a brief note that Gallery's own upstream number is used.

summary: status: pass. Phase 1 foundation is production-ready for a validation spike: 0 critical, 0 high, 3 medium, 5 low findings. Module boundary is clean (zero Compose/Activity/ViewModel imports in :core-runtime). Production code uses only Log.e (no Log.i/w/d hits, TAC-5 green). `Model.instance: Any?` anti-pattern is contained in Gallery-extracted Model.kt under the documented D6 deviation; no new code (ModelEntry, ModelInitStatus, DefaultModelRegistry, ViewModels) reintroduces it. Shared-resource discipline holds: single-active LlmModelInstance is serialised by lifecycleMutex + stale-instance guard (T9) in DefaultModelRegistry; DownloadRepository/LlmModelHelper/ModelRegistry are @Provides @Singleton in CoreRuntimeModule; ErrorLog is @Singleton via constructor injection (M2 — matches tech-spec intent, not tech-spec's @Provides literal text). User-spec D1-D12 and tech-spec T1-T11 are all implemented or covered by an approved User-Spec Deviation (D6). The three medium findings are functional-quality improvements that do not block Phase 1: M1 — download-worker failures do not reach errors.log (the `download` ErrorLog component is used for startup/allowlist paths, but not actual worker FAILED states; AC-16 is желательный so this is non-blocking); M2 — ErrorLog binding pattern differs from tech-spec Architecture text (functionally equivalent singleton); M3 — Gallery notification path uses Log.e instead of ErrorLog for one ClassNotFoundException branch. All five low findings are maintainability/Phase-2-debt items. No critical/high findings means `status: pass` per task resolution rule; the orchestrator can skip the fixer-task.
